claims.title: Parser-quality warnings can block unsafe materialization
claims.summary: The proof packet shows a reference-bearing synthetic chunk with a missing expected script warning and a quality action policy that blocks vector and graph materialization.

evidence.description: Warning count and warning details include reference_unit_missing_expected_script.
The affected chunk carries quality_action_policy with vector and graph projection disabled.

claims.explanation: The public artifacts are enough to inspect the warning, affected reference, and materialization policy without relying on private local data.
limitations: The corpus is synthetic and demonstrates behavior shape, not parser recall over a real corpus.

claims.title: Retrieval, graph, and reranker evidence remain inspectable as public artifacts
claims.summary: The proof packet includes retrieval traces, graph projection state, and reranker traces linked back to synthetic source chunks and warning evidence.

evidence.description: Retrieval run includes sources, chunk traces, timings, and static-fixture token metadata.
Graph projection artifact shows projected and blocked reference nodes.
Reranker trace artifact links candidates to parser-quality and chunk artifacts.

claims.explanation: The public packet carries the evidence states a static viewer can render later, with each state tied to redacted artifact paths.
limitations: Trace scores and timings are fixture values and are not benchmark or model-performance claims.

claims.title: Ragstudio handles 2000+ page scale benchmarks
claims.summary: Large-scale document performance remains a future benchmark until measured with public artifacts.
limitations: No 2000+ page public run artifact exists in Phase 1.
missing_evidence: A measured 2000+ page run with redacted parser, chunk, retrieval, graph, and reranker artifacts.
A validation result that proves artifact hashes and redaction status for the scale packet.
planned_proof_path: Create a separate public scale proof packet after the v1 static packet is stable.
Run validator and publish measured artifacts before changing this claim to proven.

claims.title: Canonical retrieval architecture metadata remains visible across retrieval lanes
claims.summary: Ragstudio keeps canonical chunk identity, quality policy, layout provenance, and context breadcrumbs visible across retrieval lanes before answer assembly.
limitations: Retrieval Architecture Limitations apply until public static fixtures include exported traces for every retrieval lane.
missing_evidence: A public retrieval-run artifact with canonical identity, quality policy, layout provenance, and breadcrumb fields visible across metadata, vector, graph, runtime, reranker, and context assembly traces.
A validation result proving those exported traces are public-safe and hash-covered in the proof packet.
planned_proof_path: Extend the synthetic static fixtures to include the new retrieval architecture trace fields.
Run proof validation and update this claim to proven only after public artifacts carry the trace evidence.

claims.title: Visitors can upload documents to a public Ragstudio demo
claims.summary: Public upload is intentionally disabled for v1 because it needs abuse, cost, provider, and private-content controls beyond this launch.
limitations: The v1 public site is static and must not call a live backend.
claims.disabled_reason: A public upload sandbox would introduce authentication, quota, provider-egress, abuse, and private-content risks that are explicitly out of scope for v1.
